name: Tdarr
description: Tdarr is a distributed transcoding system for automating media library management. It processes your media files in parallel across multiple nodes, supporting both CPU and GPU (NVENC/VAAPI/QSV) transcoding with FFmpeg and HandBrake.

paths.name: Server Data
paths.description: Tdarr Server database, plugins, and samples.

paths.name: Configs
paths.description: Server and Node configuration files (Tdarr_Server_Config.json, Tdarr_Node_Config.json).

paths.name: Logs
paths.description: Tdarr Server and Node log files.

paths.name: Media Library
paths.description: Your media library (movies, TV shows). Must match Radarr/Sonarr paths.

paths.name: Transcode Cache
paths.description: Temporary transcode cache directory. Use fast storage (SSD recommended).

ports.name: Server Port
ports.description: Tdarr Server communication port (used by nodes to connect).

variables.name: Server IP
variables.description: Tdarr Server bind address (0.0.0.0 for all interfaces).

variables.name: WebUI Port
variables.description: Web interface port for Tdarr dashboard.

variables.name: Internal Node
variables.description: Enable the Tdarr Node inside the Server container (recommended for single-node setups).

variables.name: In Container
variables.description: Set to true when running inside Docker.

variables.name: Node Name
variables.description: Name for the internal Tdarr Node.

variables.name: Authentication
variables.description: Enable password protection for the web UI (set to true and configure in the UI).

variables.name: NVIDIA Driver Capabilities
variables.description: NVIDIA GPU capabilities for NVENC transcoding. Set to 'all' when using NVIDIA GPU.

variables.name: NVIDIA Visible Devices
variables.description: Which NVIDIA GPUs to expose to the container. Set to 'all' or specific GPU UUID.

devices.name: DRI (VAAPI/QSV)
devices.description: GPU device for Intel/AMD VAAPI or Intel QSV hardware transcoding. Remove if using NVIDIA only.

gpus.description: NVIDIA GPU for NVENC hardware transcoding. Requires NVIDIA Container Toolkit on host. Remove if using Intel/AMD only.
